title: erigeron glabellus var. asper
intro: Erigeron glabellus var. asper, commonly known as the rough-fleabane, is a perennial plant species within the daisy family (Asteraceae) and is native to North America. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, derived from its leaves and flowers. The health benefits of Erigeron glabellus var. asper include its use in treating wounds, reducing fever, and alleviating respiratory issues such as bronchitis and coughs. Herbal preparations of the plant can be made into teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to harness its medicinal properties for various health conditions.
